title: cassiope lycopodioides var. cristapilosa
intro: Cassiope lycopodioides var. cristapilosa, also known as the hairpine or hairy club moss, is a plant species in the family Ericaceae. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial activities, making it beneficial for wound healing and skin issues. The plant is also believed to have health benefits such as reducing fever and alleviating respiratory problems. Cassiope lycopodioides var. cristapilosa can be prepared into various herbal remedies, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to treat a range of health conditions.
